zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Chapman于1569年9月21日出生在英国賀特福爾姆 哈達哈姆时，他的父亲John Chapman27岁，母亲Maud Graynge17岁。他和Mary P Gray至少有1个儿子。他去世在英国，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Chapman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a merchant or trader, Middle English chap(pe)man, chepman, Old English cēapmann, cēpemann, a compound of cēap ‘barter, bargain, price, property’ + mann ‘man’.
Jewish: adopted probably for a like-sounding or like-meaning name in some other European language; see for example   Kaufman  .

History: This name was brought independently to North America from England by numerous different bearers from the 17th century onward. John Chapmen (sic) was one of the free planters who assented to the ‘Fundamental Agreement’ of the New Haven Colony on June 4, 1639.

variants.name: Kaufman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Chaim, Emanuel, Moshe, Eytan, Herschel, Zvi, Ari, Aron.
Americanized form of German   Kaufmann  , and a variant of the same Jewish surname.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the personal name Kaufman meaning ‘merchant’. Compare Koyfman 2.

variants.name: Chipman
meanings: English: from Middle English chipman (Old English cȳp(e)mann), a side form of chapman, chepman ‘merchant, trader’; see Chapman , with which this name was interchangeable.

variants.name: Copeman
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): patronymic from the early Middle English personal name Cop(e)man, an Anglicized form of Old Scandinavian Kaupmaðr. It is a transferred use of Old Scandinavian kaupmaðr (Old Danish køpman) ‘merchant’, cognate with Old English cēapmann (see Chapman ), but there is no evidence for the occupational term in Middle English.
Americanized form of North German   Koopmann   or Dutch   Koopman  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Chapman was born on 21 September 1569, in Much Hadham, Hertfordshire, England, his father, John Chapman, was 27 and his mother, Maud Graynge, was 17. He had at least 1 son with Mary P Gray. He died in England, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Chapman est né le 21 septembre 1569, à Much Hadham, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Chapman, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Maud Graynge, avait 17 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mary P Gray. Il est décédé à Angleterre, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Chapman am 21. September 1569 in Much Hadham, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Chapman 27 und seine Mutter Maud Graynge 17. Er hatte mit Mary P Gray mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb in England und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Chapman è nato il il 21 settembre 1569 nel Much Hadham, Hertfordshire, England, il suo padre, John Chapman, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Maud Graynge, aveva 17 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mary P Gray. Morì nel England, e fu sepolto a England.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Chapmanが1569年9月21日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアムチ ハドハムで生まれた時，父John Chapmanは27歳，母Maud Grayngeは17歳であった。彼はMary P Grayとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼はイギリスで死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Chapman(이)가 1569년 9월 21일 영국 허트퍼드실르 무치 하드함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Chapman(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud Graynge(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Mary P Gray(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국에서 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Chapman nasceu em 21 de setembro de 1569, em Much Hadham, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Chapman, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Maud Graynge, tinha 17 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mary P Gray. Ele faleceu em Inglaterra, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Chapman родился 21 сентября 1569 г. в местности муч хадхам, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Chapman было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Graynge, 17. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mary P Gray. Он умер в местности Англия и был похоронен в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Chapman nació el 21 de septiembre de 1569, en Much Hadham, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Chapman, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Maud Graynge, tenía 17 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Mary P Gray. Murió en Inglaterra, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra.
